phase1.output:
Enter withdrawal amount: $500
Withdrew $500.0. New balance: $500.0
--- Propagating exception ---
Caught propagated exception: Risky operation failed!

--- OR ---

Enter withdrawal amount: $1500
Error: Insufficient balance! Balance: $1000.0
--- Propagating exception ---
Caught propagated exception: Risky operation failed!

--- OR ---

Enter withdrawal amount: $-50
Illegal argument: Amount cannot be negative!
--- Propagating exception ---
Caught propagated exception: Risky operation failed!
phase1.explanation:
throw and throws are used to work with exceptions:

throw (inside method):
• Actively CREATES and THROWS an exception
• Example: throw new Exception("Something went wrong");
• Can throw any Throwable
• Stops execution immediately

throws (method signature):
• DECLARES that a method might throw an exception
• Example: void method() throws Exception
• Tells caller to handle it
• Can list multiple exceptions: throws IOException, SQLException

When to use throw:
• Validation failures
• Invalid state
• Custom business logic errors

When to use throws:
• When you can't handle the exception yourself
• When you want the caller to handle it
• For checked exceptions

Propagation:
• Exception propagates up the call stack
• Each caller can either catch it or declare it with throws
• If main throws, program crashes

Key rule:
• Checked exceptions: must be caught or declared with throws
• Unchecked exceptions: optional to handle

phase2.errorMessage:
Validator.java:5: error: incompatible types: String cannot be converted to Throwable
            throw "Age cannot be negative!";  // Wrong!
            ^
1 error
phase2.explanation:
throw only accepts Throwable objects (Exception, Error, or their subclasses)!

You CANNOT throw a String, int, or other primitive.

FIX: throw new IllegalArgumentException("Age cannot be negative!");

Always create an exception object when throwing.

Also: provide meaningful error messages for debugging!

phase3.prompt:
Create a class Product with:
• Fields: name (String), price (double)
• Constructor with validation:
  - If name is null or empty → throw IllegalArgumentException
  - If price < 0 → throw IllegalArgumentException
• Method: getDiscountedPrice(double discount) that:
  - If discount < 0 or > 50 → throw IllegalArgumentException
  - Returns price * (1 - discount/100)

In main, test with valid and invalid inputs.
dialogueHints: Constructor: check if name is null or empty, if price < 0
phase3.openingDialogue: throw and throws — you're in control! 🎮
